steps.title: Parse the underlined claim
steps.content: The underlined sentence states: “As the movement spread beyond Chile, the breadth of musical traditions incorporated into its foundation also expanded.” The key phrase is musical traditions expanding beyond Chilean folk forms. A correct illustrating detail must show a specific non-Chilean musical tradition being incorporated into nueva canción as it spread to other countries.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Many feature political commentary addressing contemporary issues that stemmed from shared experiences of European colonization in Latin American countries.’: ✗ Addresses thematic content (political commentary) rather than the incorporation of new musical traditions.
  • Choice B — ‘Many demonstrate the stylistic influence of corrido, a genre of narrative songs from Mexico that had come to be characterized by political themes in the early 1800s.’: ✓ Corrido is a distinctly Mexican musical tradition — its influence shows the movement incorporating a new tradition from outside Chile as it spread.
  • Choice C — ‘Many were written with parts meant to be played on the quena, a traditional flute used across Andean countries, including Chile.’: ✗ The quena is described as including Chile, suggesting continuity with the original Chilean foundation rather than expansion into new traditions.
  • Choice D — ‘Many were produced by Argentinian artists in the late 1950s, with others by artists in additional Latin American countries first emerging soon after.’: ✗ Addresses artist geography, not musical tradition — having Argentine artists does not show new musical forms being incorporated.
That confirms B is correct.
